agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Session 130 -- US-258 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 21 6/10 -- B-047 US-E e2e drill).  NEW tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e.py -- 7 tests / 5 classes / ~640 LOC / @pytest.mark.integration; mocks ONLY at HTTP boundary (UpdateChecker.httpOpener) + subprocess boundary (UpdateApplier.subprocessRun); real production classes drive every assertion (no internal-state monkeypatching).  TestSelfUpdateE2EHappyPath: server NEWER -> check writes marker -> apply runs priorRef + fetch + checkout + dry-run + deploy + post-verify in order -> .deploy-version reflects target -> marker cleared -> SUCCESS; phase ordering pinned by index assertion on the recorded subprocess sequence.  TestSelfUpdateE2EDeployFailureTriggersRollback (x2): full-deploy failure (rc=1 via _FailFullDeployRunner argv-length discriminator) fires rollback chain (`git checkout abc1234` + `sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd`) + clears marker; dry-run failure short-circuits BEFORE the full deploy ever runs.  TestSelfUpdateE2EDriveStateGate: stale (deferred-apply) marker SURVIVES the drive intact; checker skips HTTP entirely; applier returns SKIPPED_DRIVING with ZERO subprocesses -- pins the deferred-apply convention.  TestSelfUpdateE2EUpToDate: server SAME version -> no marker -> apply NO_MARKER + ZERO subprocesses.  TestSelfUpdateE2EWireShape: X-API-Key + GET /api/v1/release/current invariant audit through the integrated path.  specs/architecture.md Section 11 gained 'Pi Self-Update Lifecycle (B-047 US-A through US-E, Sprints 19-21)' subsection between Release Versioning + Wake-on-Power: ASCII diagram of the two-process pipeline, marker-as-only-inter-step-channel rule, safety-gate ordering rationale (drive-state -> power-source -> recent-OBD -> applyEnabled, with applyEnabled deliberately AFTER safety so logs show 'would have been safe to apply'), priorRef-and-rollback contract, marker-cleared-on-every-terminal-outcome poisoned-target invariant, e2e coverage table, mod history.  Verification: pytest tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e.py -v -- 7/7 PASS in 13.49s; pytest tests/pi/integration/ -q -- 47 passed / 7 skipped in 55.95s (zero adjacent regressions); ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors / 21 informational sizing-cap warnings.  PRE-EXISTING TD-042 acknowledged out-of-scope (commit 57bdda6 theme-field addition broke 24 unit tests in tests/pi/update/ + tests/server/; my e2e fixture uses the current contract so 7/7 pass cleanly without touching the broken upstream tests).  GOTCHAS recorded: (a) argv-length discriminator pattern for full-deploy-vs-dry-run failure scripting -- substring matching can't separate them because both contain 'deploy-pi.sh'; subclass _FailFullDeployRunner overrides __call__ for the 2-arg case.  (b) Fake runner must simulate deploy-pi.sh's .deploy-version stamp side-effect (deployStampPath + deployStampVersion) when the full deploy command runs (NOT --dry-run), otherwise post-deploy verify reads stale local version and fails the integration test even though the apply state machine is correct.  (c) sprint.json individual records are now too large for clean Edit-tool editing -- write a small one-shot Python script (then delete) for closeout bookkeeping.  This drill is the integration-readiness gate before flipping pi.update.applyEnabled=true in production per US-258 intent.
